source.description: Six-panel raster contact sheet generated for the July 5, 2026 Apple Turnover Day sketch lesson, reviewed before cropping.
source.notes: Prompt required one consistent plated turnover from construction shapes to finished pencil sketch, with no embedded labels or decorative UI.

process_strategy: The sequence builds the plate ellipse, triangular pastry, crimped edge, apple filling slit, fork, crumbs, and warm shading before the final panel only darkens keeper lines and adds restrained color.

final_elements.name: round plate ellipse
final_elements.description: A shallow oval plate underneath the pastry.

final_elements.name: triangular turnover body
final_elements.description: A folded triangular pastry sitting on the plate.

final_elements.name: rounded folded seam
final_elements.description: Softened pastry corners and the folded seam running along the turnover.

final_elements.name: crimped pastry edge
final_elements.description: Small pressed scallops along the folded edge.

final_elements.name: apple filling slit
final_elements.description: A short opening near the top with a small peek of apple filling.

final_elements.name: fork beside the plate
final_elements.description: A simple fork angled beside the plate.

final_elements.name: crumbs on the plate
final_elements.description: Small broken pastry crumbs near the turnover.

final_elements.name: warm pastry shading
final_elements.description: Restrained golden colored-pencil shading on the pastry top.

frames.visible_job: Blocks in the plate ellipse and a light triangular turnover body.
Rounds the pastry corners and draws the folded seam over the construction.
Adds crimp marks along the folded edge and cleans the plate rim.
Adds a small apple filling slit and a fork beside the plate.
Adds crumbs on the plate and light warm shading over the already-drawn pastry.

final_step.visible_job: Darkens keeper contours, sharpens the crimp marks, warms the pastry shading, and clarifies the fork and crumbs already present.

rejection_checks: No plate, fork, filling slit, crimping, crumbs, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
